name: The World of Solo Leveling
description: A comprehensive lorebook covering the hunters, monsters, guilds, dungeons, and story arcs of the Solo Leveling universe. Includes full canonical coverage from the completed webnovel with webtoon and anime adaptations.

1.content: The Solo Leveling universe is a modern fantasy world where magical gates began appearing approximately ten years before the main story, allowing hunters to access dungeon realms filled with dangerous magic beasts. The Gate System provides a structured hierarchy of dungeons ranked from E-Rank to S-Rank, with powerful hunters—also ranked on the same scale—ascending into national-level importance and international geopolitical influence. Hunters possess the ability to sense mana and evolve through specialized awakening, granting them superhuman combat abilities that vary by rank and specialization. The central conflict revolves around an ancient cosmic war between the Rulers (eight transcendent beings of light) and the Monarchs (nine transcendent beings of darkness), who engineered the gate system as preparation for their invasion of the human world—a conflict that places humanity in existential jeopardy and shapes the world's destiny through the emergence of the Shadow Monarch and his heir.
1.name: Solo Leveling Universe

2.content: The Absolute Being was an ancient god and the creator of both the Monarchs and the Rulers. At the dawn of time, the Absolute Being split light to create the Rulers and split darkness to create the Monarchs, setting them to war against each other as mere entertainment for himself. A cruel and self-centered individual, the Absolute Being viewed his creations only as tools for his own amusement and showed poor judgment by failing to anticipate the Rulers' rebellion. Seven of the eight Rulers, realizing that their god sought only to prolong their endless war rather than grant them victory, rose against the Absolute Being and murdered him in his throne room, spearing him through with their combined might. Despite his immense creative power and ability to forge objects of otherworldly power like the Cup of Reincarnation, the Absolute Being proved incapable of combat and perished without struggle.
2.name: Absolute Being

3.content: The Ahjin Guild is the strongest guild in Korea and the most powerful guild in the world. Unlike most top guilds in other countries, the guild has only three official members with only one S-Rank hunter among them. However, when Sung Jinwoo's shadow soldiers are included, the Ahjin Guild surpasses every other guild in existence in both strength and numbers. Sung Jinwoo serves as the Guild Master, Yoo Jinho as Vice-Guild Master, and Yoo Soohyun as a guild member. Jinwoo's shadow soldiers, an army of powerful summons that rival S-Rank hunters in combat capability, give the Ahjin Guild military dominance over all other organizations in the world.
3.name: Ahjin Guild

4.content: The Ant King was the hidden boss of the Jeju Island S-Rank Gate and one of the most powerful magic beasts in existence. A large humanoid ant with a black exoskeleton, glowing red eyes, and four transparent wings, the Ant King possessed human-level intelligence and exceptional strategic ability that made him far more dangerous than his hive. The Ant King killed eight S-Rank Hunters during the fourth Jeju Island Raid, including Goto Ryuji, the strongest hunter in Japan, before encountering Sung Jinwoo. After realizing he could not defeat Jinwoo despite employing every ability in his arsenal, the Ant King attempted to flee but was killed by Jinwoo's Mutilation skill. Jinwoo later revived the Ant King as a shadow soldier and renamed him Beru.
4.name: Ant King

5.content: Antares is the strongest of the nine Monarchs and the Dragon King, possessing power rivaled only by Ashborn among all sentient beings. As the leader of the Monarchs' invasion of the human world, Antares orchestrated widespread destruction across multiple continents, personally wiping out Canada and advancing toward North America with unstoppable force. Antares welcomed Ashborn back to the Monarchs' ranks after Ashborn's earlier conflict with his brethren, recognizing that two Monarchs had been lost to the Rulers and the remaining seven were greatly weakened. During the final battle, Antares engaged Sung Jinwoo in direct combat after Jinwoo had claimed Ashborn's full powers, with their fight ultimately determining the fate of both the Monarchs and humanity. Jinwoo defeated Antares through superior adaptability and the combined powers of the Shadow Monarch, though in the original timeline this required assistance from the Rulers. In the revised timeline where Jinwoo spent 27 years in constant warfare, Jinwoo ultimately killed Antares completely by himself without external support, demonstrating his ascendance beyond even the greatest Monarchs.
5.name: Antares

6.content: Ashborn was the King of the Dead and the Monarch of Shadows, the strongest being in existence before his death. Eons ago, Ashborn fought alongside the Rulers against the Monarchs at the Absolute Being's command, remaining the only loyal Ruler when his brethren rebelled. His power was so fearful that even the Monarchs Rakan and Baran conspired against him, though Ashborn killed Baran in retaliation. After centuries of conflict, Ashborn sought Sung Jinwoo as the only human worthy of inheriting his powers. In the Double Dungeon, Ashborn betrayed his partner Kandiaru and transferred his black heart to Jinwoo, choosing him as the next Shadow Monarch. When Jinwoo was killed by Rakan, Ashborn manifested an illusory realm explaining his history and gave Jinwoo the choice to fight or rest before reviving him with full Shadow Monarch powers. Ashborn's control over shadows, gate creation, and shapeshifting made him nearly unrivaled except by Antares.
6.name: Ashborn

7.content: Baran is one of the nine Monarchs and a creature of immense power whose full capabilities were never fully displayed before his death. When Ashborn's overwhelming strength earned fear from both Rulers and Monarchs alike, Baran conspired with the Beast Monarch Rakan to attack and eliminate Ashborn, viewing his power as a threat to Monarch supremacy. Ashborn easily defeated Baran in their confrontation and killed him in retaliation for his treachery, crushing Baran's skull with a single hand despite Baran's status as a Monarch. Baran's corpse served as a source of power for Ashborn's shadow extraction ability, though the cost of Baran's death severely depleted Ashborn's shadow army in the process. Baran's attempt to eliminate the most powerful being suggests he possessed considerable strength, yet proved no match for Ashborn's overwhelming might. Few details of Baran's personal history or abilities survive, as he was eliminated early in recorded conflicts between Monarchs and Rulers.
7.name: Baran

8.content: Cha Hae-In is a Korean S-Rank hunter, Vice-Guild Master of the Hunters Guild, and the wife of Sung Jinwoo. A former track athlete whose career ended from a severe ankle injury, Hae-In awakened as a hunter and rose to become the 9th S-Rank Hunter in Korea through her mastery of swordplay and meticulous approach to dungeon raiding. Hae-In is uniquely sensitive to mana's smell, a trait that makes other hunters reek to her, though she found Sung Jinwoo's scent pleasant from their first meeting. Known among her guild as "The Dancer" for her graceful sword movements, she developed romantic feelings for Jinwoo after he saved her life during the Jeju Island Raid, though she nearly died from the Ant King's fatal blow. Hae-In joined Jinwoo's Ahjin Guild despite her prestigious position and eventually became his wife, giving birth to their son Sung Suho. Her exceptional combat abilities, including her powerful Sword Dance and Sword of Light techniques, made her one of the strongest S-Rank hunters in Korea.
8.name: Cha Hae-In

9.content: The Cup of Reincarnation is a large ornamental chalice with yellow trims and indigo-colored glass created by the Absolute Being with the power to rewind time ten years with each activation. After the Rulers murdered the Absolute Being and assumed his role as gods, they seized his tools including the Cup and used it to reset the timeline when their initial attempts to prevent the Monarchs from destroying Earth failed. The Rulers later discovered that the cup's power was not infinite and grew increasingly depleted with each use, eventually forcing them to resort to creating Gates to saturate Earth with mana instead. After defeating Antares, Sung Jinwoo requested the Rulers use the Cup one final time so he could rescue everyone lost during the Monarchs War and prevent the timeline's destruction. The Rulers complied, fully exhausting the Cup's remaining power and sending all parties ten years into the past, allowing Jinwoo to slay the Monarchs before they could cause the apocalypse.
9.name: Cup of Reincarnation

10.content: The Demon Castle was a S-Rank instant dungeon created by the Architect Kandiaru and inhabited by demons. The dungeon appeared as a destroyed version of Seoul littered with ruined buildings and engulfed in flames, featuring a total of 100 floors with four bosses. As revealed by Ashborn, the Shadow Monarch, the Demon Castle's appearance was based on an alternate timeline version of Seoul in which the war between the Rulers and Monarchs devastated the human world and wiped out all of mankind. The four bosses Sung Jinwoo faced were Cerberus on the first floor, Vulcan on the 50th floor, Metus on the 75th floor, and Baran the Demon King on the 100th floor. After Jinwoo cleared all 100 floors and returned to the human world, the Demon Castle was restored to its original state.
10.name: Demon Castle

11.content: Dragons are a race of draconic beings ruled by Antares, Monarch of Destruction and King of Dragons. Much like mythical dragons, they are large serpent-like creatures depicted variously as winged and four-legged, wingless serpentine creatures with above-average intelligence, or possessing humanoid appearances in ancient-class and sky dragon forms. Dragons are possibly the strongest and most powerful magic beasts, capable of flight, overwhelming physical prowess, high magical resistance, and the ability to unleash powerful torrents of fire. The first dragon to appear on Earth, Kamish, managed to destroy part of the West Coast of the USA and kill thousands of hunters before being defeated. In the revised timeline after Jinwoo's temporal reset, all dragons except one were hunted to extinction by the Shadow Monarch to prevent them from threatening the universe.
11.name: Dragons

12.content: The Final Battle Arc represented the climax of Sung Jinwoo's journey as he faced his greatest challenge yet. Having fully absorbed the powers of the Shadow Monarch through the System's trials, Jinwoo prepared for a fight with Antares, the strongest Monarch and the King of Dragons. This battle would determine the fate of humanity itself, as the Monarchs sought to hunt down the Rulers' vessels and the humans they inhabited. Antares posed the greatest threat to Jinwoo's victory and represented the ultimate test of his power and resolve. The outcome of this final confrontation would reshape the entire world and alter the course of history for both humans and magical beings.
12.name: Final Battle Arc

13.content: Gates are magical portals that form a bridge between the human world and enigmatic dungeon realms filled with magic beasts. Gates began appearing in the human world approximately ten years before the main story's events as a result of the Rulers' strategy to saturate Earth with mana, preparing it to withstand the brunt of their war against the Monarchs. Ranked on the S-E scale alongside hunters and magic beasts, gates appear seemingly at random from highways to abandoned areas to schools, and remain open until their dungeon boss is defeated. If not cleared within days, a Dungeon Break occurs, releasing the magic beasts from within into the world. Red Gates represent a rare variant that only manifest in B-Rank or higher gates and seal off their entrances after a critical threshold of people enter, creating altered environments where time moves faster than in the outside world.
13.name: Gates

14.content: Go Gunhee was a Korean S-Rank hunter and Chairman of the Korean Hunters Association, serving as one of the most respected figures in Korean society. Originally a corporate chairman, Gunhee awakened as a hunter and easily became Korea's strongest, but his advanced age forced him to retire from active hunting after only a few years despite healing magic's discovery. Humble and open-minded, Gunhee showed great appreciation for those with good morals, particularly Sung Jinwoo, and worked tirelessly to support Korea's hunter interests despite his deteriorating health. When the Monarchs invaded the human world, Gunhee fought Sillad to buy time for Jinwoo to arrive, deliberately breaking through the barrier around him to alert Jinwoo for help, though he had already lost too much blood to survive. In the new timeline created by the Cup of Reincarnation, Gunhee never became a hunter and lived a normal life, only learning of his former timeline when Jinwoo secretly visited him in the hospital with Holy Water of Life that extended his life by ten additional years before his peaceful death.
14.name: Go Gunhee

15.content: The Gold Dragon Guild was a major guild in China that was completely wiped out in a battle against an Arch Lich. The guild's failure to kill the Arch Lich caused a dungeon break that would have developed into a total catastrophe had it not been for Liu Zhigang, who stepped in to handle the situation and killed the Lich on his own. Beyond this notable incident, details regarding the Gold Dragon Guild, including its members and the full extent of its history, remain largely obscure.
15.name: Gold Dragon Guild

16.content: The Hunters Guild is the strongest and largest guild amongst the top five Korean guilds and is active in the Seoul Metropolitan Area. The guild is the only guild in South Korea to have two S-Rank hunters, with Choi Jong-In serving as Guild Master and Cha Hae-In serving as Vice-Guild Master. Due to being the largest guild in South Korea, the guild operates two strike squads instead of just one. The first squad is led by Choi Jong-In and Cha Hae-In and consists entirely of A-Rank hunters. The second squad is led by Son Kihoon and consists of both A-Rank and B-Rank hunters. The guild also operates a mining team led by Bae Yoon-Suk that works on A-Rank dungeon raids.
16.name: Hunters Guild

17.content: The Jeju Island Arc marked the major turning point in Sung Jinwoo's life and career. With his mother finally cured of Eternal Slumber through the Holy Water of Life, Jinwoo initially intended to sit out of the Jeju Island raid to care for her. However, he was forced to change his mind when a monstrous ant, the Ant King, appeared during the raid and began slaughtering S-Rank hunters left and right. Jinwoo arrived on the island to find the raid nearly failing and eight of the 16 S-Ranks participating already dead. Jinwoo engaged the Ant King in a lengthy battle and ultimately defeated it, saving his allies and successfully clearing the gate. His victory made him a national hero in Korea and garnered attention from powerful nations like America and China, fundamentally shifting the geopolitical balance of power in the world.
17.name: Jeju Island Arc

18.content: The Jeju Island S-Rank Gate spawned on Jeju Island about four years before the events of the main storyline. The Korean Hunters Association attempted multiple search-and-destroy missions to clear the gate but ultimately failed, resulting in a dungeon break seven days later that released ant monsters into the human world and killed thousands of civilians, turning the island into a wasteland. Over two years, the Korean government launched two more failed missions and formally abandoned efforts to reclaim Jeju. The ants eventually evolved by growing wings, enabling them to attack nearby islands and cause further civilian deaths. In response, the Draw Sword Guild collaborated with Korean hunters in a raid plan where Japanese S-Rank hunters would draw out the ants while Korean S-Rank hunters killed the Ant Queen. The raid nearly failed when the Ant King, the true boss, easily killed eight of the 16 S-Ranks participating, including Min Byung-Gyu. Sung Jinwoo arrived to save his allies and killed the Ant King after a lengthy battle, becoming a national hero in Korea and cementing his status as one of the strongest hunters in Asia.
18.name: Jeju Island S-Rank Gate

19.content: The Job Change Arc saw Sung Jinwoo summoned by the System to an instant dungeon where he encountered an army of armored soldiers and a mysterious red knight that guarded an empty throne. Inside this dungeon, Jinwoo faced trials and challenges that culminated in a job change, advancing him to a new class. This arc marked a significant power progression for Jinwoo, granting him new abilities and strengthening his connection to the System. The throne room and its red knight guardian became symbolic of Jinwoo's transformation into a true monster-hunting force.
19.name: Job Change Arc

20.content: Liu Zhigang is the strongest hunter in China and one of the five National Level Hunters, ranking second in power only to Sung Jinwoo. A handsome young man with dark yellow eyes and black hair worn in a ponytail, Zhigang is a master swordsman wielding both traditional Chinese dao and jian blades with deadly precision. In the original timeline, Zhigang expressed disappointment at missing the Ant King fight and bluntly told reporters that Sung Jinwoo was worlds above him in strength. Zhigang gained respect for Jinwoo after witnessing him defeat giants that Zhigang struggled against. After the Cup of Reincarnation reset the timeline, Zhigang regained his powers but without his Ruler vessel status, initially becoming a 6-Star hunter before restoring his National Level status. He resisted possession attempts until Sung Suho freed him, recognizing Suho as Jinwoo's son.
20.name: Liu Zhigang

21.content: Rakan is the Beast Monarch and one of the nine Monarchs, known for his humanoid form and devastating combat capabilities. Like Baran, Rakan turned on Ashborn when the Shadow Monarch's power became too dominant, conspiring to eliminate him as a threat to Monarch independence. Ashborn defeated both Rakan and Baran simultaneously, though the effort cost him nearly his entire shadow army, demonstrating that while powerful, Rakan could not match Ashborn's unparalleled strength. Later, Rakan was sent to hunt down Sung Jinwoo in Seoul alongside other Monarchs, where he killed Jinwoo in combat by impaling him fatally, though Ashborn later revealed this as an illusion. During the revised timeline spanning 27 years, Rakan was killed by Sung Jinwoo with only four blows after Jinwoo inherited Ashborn's full powers, showcasing the massive gap between a Monarch's strength and the Shadow Monarch's might. Rakan's willingness to betray Ashborn and his combat prowess against multiple national-level hunters demonstrated his formidable position among the Monarchs.
21.name: Rakan

22.content: The Reawakening Arc began after Sung Jinwoo survived the Double Dungeon and awoke three days later in a local hospital. During his recovery, Jinwoo discovered the System, a magical program that functions like a video game and makes him exponentially stronger with every quest he completes. This discovery of the System fundamentally changed Jinwoo's life and marked the true beginning of his journey to become the world's strongest hunter. The System would grant him quests, allow him to level up, and eventually grant him shadow soldiers to command.
22.name: Reawakening Arc

23.content: Red Gates are special gates that completely seal off their entrance once humans pass through, trapping hunters already inside and preventing anyone from outside from entering. The lowest difficulty level a Red Gate can present is B-Rank, making trapped hunters face dire situations unless they have support from at least one A-Rank hunter. The environment within Red Gates is harsh and ranges from scorching deserts to freezing snowy fields, causing hunters to face death not only from monsters but also from environmental factors such as frostbite, heatstroke, and starvation. Red Gates are impossible to predict because they initially appear as normal blue-colored gates and only reveal their true nature after humans pass through them. Time is dilated inside Red Gates, with one day inside equaling one hour in the human world. Red Gates stop radiating Mana after closing, preventing accurate measurement of their mana levels.
23.name: Red Gate

24.content: The Red Gate Arc occurred when Sung Jinwoo attempted to expose Han Song-Yi, a newbie E-Rank hunter, to the dangerous reality of being a hunter. Jinwoo reached out to his contacts in the White Tiger Guild and managed to get both Song-Yi and himself spots in one of the guild's training exercises. However, things took a horrible turn when the gate used for the exercise revealed itself to be a Red Gate, stranding them on a freezing, snow-covered landscape. Due to poor long-term decisions made by raid party leader Kim Chul, eight of the 12 hunters who went through the gate were killed by monsters or environmental factors. Chul himself was later murdered by Jinwoo when he went violently insane and attacked the low-ranking hunters who had remained in the forest. As a result, only three of the 12 guild members came back alive.
24.name: Red Gate Arc

25.content: Sung Il-Hwan is the father of Sung Jinwoo and one of Korea's first S-Rank hunters, though his strength was never officially recognized before the ranking system existed. A former firefighter who married Park Kyung-Hye and had two children, Il-Hwan abandoned his family to become a hunter when magic beasts were discovered, eventually rising to extraordinary power. Ten years before the main story, Il-Hwan became trapped inside a dungeon and was rescued by the Rulers, who granted him their power and tasked him with killing the new Shadow Monarch soon to be born. Upon learning that Jinwoo himself was the Shadow Monarch, Il-Hwan could not bring himself to complete his mission and instead switched to protecting his son from the other Monarchs. During the Monarchs War, Il-Hwan fought both Sillad and Rakan simultaneously, severing his left arm in the battle but holding them back long enough for Jinwoo to claim the full Shadow Monarch powers. Il-Hwan died shortly after from overexerting powers his body could not sustain, finally reuniting with Jinwoo and apologizing for his long absence before his body dissolved into shards of light.
25.name: Sung Il-Hwan

26.content: Sung Jinwoo is the strongest hunter in the world and the second Shadow Monarch, who inherited the position from Ashborn. Originally an infamously weak E-Rank hunter before being chosen as the Player of the System, Jinwoo rose to unmatched power by exploiting the System's unique growth mechanics without restraint. Jinwoo's most astonishing ability is his explosive growth rate, allowing him to become massively stronger with every battle. After defeating all nine Monarchs and their forces across a 27-year conflict in a revised timeline, Jinwoo possesses absolute control over shadows and darkness, immeasurable strength and speed, combat mastery refined through countless encounters, and agelessness as a consequence of inheriting Ashborn's powers. Jinwoo founded the Ahjin Guild and married Cha Hae-In, with whom he had a son named Sung Suho. His core motivation throughout his journey has been protecting his family, even at the cost of becoming humanity's most powerful force.
26.name: Sung Jinwoo

27.content: The System is a magical program designed by Kandiaru to develop a human vessel capable of absorbing the Shadow Monarch Ashborn's powers at their maximum potential. The System selected Sung Jinwoo as its Player after he voluntarily sacrificed himself in the Double Dungeon, enabling him to grow stronger at an exponential rate through quests, skill acquisition, stat distribution, and an unlimited inventory system. The System's video game interface was deliberately designed to allow Jinwoo to understand its mechanics without confusion, and it automatically protected him by activating passive skills when necessary and alerting him to nearby threats. Originally created with the singular purpose of preparing Jinwoo as Ashborn's vessel, the System was automatically deleted after Ashborn transferred his full powers to Jinwoo and his purpose was fulfilled. In the revised timeline, Jinwoo modified and passed the System to his son Sung Suho, with the modified version proving capable of independent adaptation and learning.
27.name: System

28.content: Thomas Andre is the strongest hunter in America and one of the five National Level Hunters, ranking first in the world before Sung Jinwoo's emergence. A towering, muscular man with blonde hair and red eyes, Thomas rose from poverty through brute strength and fighting prowess, eventually becoming a hunter when magic beasts were discovered. Thomas obtained Kamish's Wrath, a pair of daggers crafted from a dragon's fang, after defeating the dragon Kamish in battle, though he kept them unused for eight years. Though proud and aggressive, Thomas proved gracious in defeat when Jinwoo nearly killed him, and later befriended him by treating him to a meal and gifting him the powerful Kamish's Wrath daggers. During the final battle against Antares and his armies, Thomas summoned shadow soldiers granted to him by Jinwoo to fight alongside him. After the Rulers reset the timeline, Thomas became a professional wrestling champion in the new world, though he later regained his hunter powers and ascended to Monarch status.
28.name: Thomas Andre

29.content: The White Tiger Guild is one of the top five Korean guilds active in the Seoul Metropolitan Area. The guild practices training new recruits by having them go on raids with stronger members into low-ranking gates. The White Tiger Guild is divided into management departments, each with its own work and responsibilities. The First Management Department, led by Chief Joo Sungchan, manages official hunters and guild members. The Second Management Department, run by Ahn Sangmin, handles recruiting new hunters and their training. The guild's Guild Master is Baek Yoonho, an S-Rank fighter, and the Vice-Guild Master is Baek Miho. The guild maintains a territory in the city where gates open and reserves gates for internships and training of recruits, protecting them with B or A-Rank guild masters.
29.name: White Tiger Guild

30.content: The Ahjin Guild Arc marked Sung Jinwoo's transition from a lone hunter to a guild master. Jinwoo founded the Ahjin Guild and agreed to assist the Knights Guild in clearing a high-ranking A-Rank gate in exchange for payment. Simultaneously, a supernatural crisis erupted at the high school where Jinwoo's sister Jinah was a student, forcing Jinwoo to divide his attention between guild operations and protecting his family. This arc highlighted the escalating stakes of Jinwoo's power, as his actions now affected entire organizations and left his family vulnerable to threats that only he could handle.
30.name: Ahjin Guild Arc

31.content: Ants are intelligent hive-based creatures that emerged from a dungeon on Jeju Island and posed a catastrophic threat to Asia. Ants range from worker ants to powerful officers commanded by an Ant King of enormous strength. Ants demonstrate intelligence, organization, and the ability to communicate through a hierarchical structure. Ants attack various Japanese cities on the south coast following the dungeon break on Jeju Island. Ants are capable of devastating raids requiring the combined efforts of multiple S-Rank hunters to contain.
31.name: Ants

32.content: The Asura Guild is a major national guild in South Korea that ranks among the top five Korean guilds. The guild specializes in coordinated raid operations and maintains a large force of A-Rank and B-Rank hunters capable of handling challenging dungeon breaks and gate clearance missions. The Asura Guild competes with other top guilds like the Hunters Guild, White Tiger Guild, and others for territory, resources, and prestige within South Korea's hunter hierarchy.
32.name: Asura Guild

33.content: Baek Yoonho is a Korean S-Rank Hunter and the Guild Master of the White Tiger Guild. Baek Yoonho is a muscular young man with spiky orange hair and orange eyes with pronounced canine teeth, whose eyes glow yellow and hair turns white when using his powers. Baek Yoonho is ambitious and serious, deeply caring for his guild members and notably perceptive, being the first to realize Sung Jinwoo had no limit to his strength. Baek Yoonho can transform into a tiger-like humanoid monster with white fur, sharp claws, and prominent fangs that significantly increases his strength and speed. Baek Yoonho possesses the Eyes of the Beast ability to measure power levels and has shown great respect and tactical wisdom in the revised timeline.
33.name: Baek Yoonho

34.content: The Black Tortoise Guild is one of South Korea's top five national guilds and maintains a significant presence in the Seoul Metropolitan Area. The guild operates multiple hunting teams and specializes in specific types of dungeon operations and gate management. The Black Tortoise Guild competes with other top-tier Korean guilds for dominance and resources, participating in major national operations and dungeon clearing efforts throughout South Korea.
34.name: Black Tortoise Guild

35.content: Cartenon Temple is an infamous dungeon where a Double Dungeon incident occurred, trapping an E-Rank hunter and an A-Rank hunter with two separate boss creatures. The temple's walls crumbled and collapsed during the hunters' attempt to escape, crushing both the E-Rank hunter and the A-Rank hunter's arm. Sung Jinwoo, at the time a weak E-Rank hunter desperate for money, was hired to join a raid team attempting to clear the dungeon. The incident at Cartenon Temple would mark the turning point of Jinwoo's life, as he miraculously survived despite being told that only the A-Rank hunter would survive the collapse.
35.name: Cartenon Temple

36.content: Chaos World is a dimensional realm and instant dungeon created through arcane magic, containing multiple floors filled with powerful monsters and treasure. The dungeon serves as a testing ground where hunters can gain experience, acquire magical items, and face dangerous opponents in a controlled supernatural environment. Chaos World dungeons are highly sought after by elite hunters seeking to increase their power, as monsters within are significantly stronger than standard dungeon creatures and drop valuable loot unavailable elsewhere.
36.name: Chaos World

37.content: China is a major world power that hosts numerous A-Rank and S-Rank dungeons and maintains one of the world's largest hunter populations. The nation's most famous S-Rank hunter is Liu Zhigang, the Shadow Sovereign of China, who single-handedly defeated the Arch Lich that destroyed the Gold Dragon Guild. China's government heavily invests in hunter organizations and military strength, making it a significant player in the international hunting community and a key ally and rival of other major nations.
37.name: China

38.content: The Chivalry Guild is a major hunter organization in South Korea that ranks among the nation's most powerful guilds. The guild maintains a substantial roster of A-Rank and B-Rank hunters and participates in significant dungeon raids and gate clearing missions. The Chivalry Guild represents one of the primary power players in South Korea's hunter market, competing with other top guilds for territory control, gate access, and prestige within the national hunting hierarchy.
38.name: Chivalry Guild

39.content: Choi Jong-In is a Korean S-Rank Hunter and the Guild Master of the Hunters Guild. Choi Jong-In is a lanky young man with red hair in a curtain hairstyle whose eyes glow bright magenta when using his powers. Choi Jong-In is the strongest mage-type hunter in Korea and is rumored to be able to destroy a tall building with a single spell. Choi Jong-In wields Fire Magic allowing him to create flame spears, flame prisons, flame dragons, and flame arrows with devastating power. Choi Jong-In possesses a massive mana pool surpassing most characters and in the revised timeline has contracted with an unnamed fire elemental appearing as a blue flaming bird.
39.name: Choi Jong-In

40.content: Christopher Reed was one of the two strongest hunters in America and one of the five National Level Hunters, ranking 3rd in the world. Christopher Reed was a tall and muscular young man with wavy brown hair, blue eyes, and slight facial hair that glowed light blue when using his powers. Christopher Reed possessed Fire Magic allowing him to create extremely powerful flames, and wielded Ruler's Authority to control objects via telekinesis. Christopher Reed died in an unseen battle against three Monarchs after being warned by Norma Selner of his impending death. The Apostle of Conquest later salvaged part of Chris's corpse to create a clone for use as a vessel.
40.name: Christopher Reed

41.content: Death Knights are powerful undead creatures that serve under Ashborn, the King of the Dead and Monarch of Shadows. Death Knights are skeletal humanoid warriors with supernatural characteristics. Death Knights are summoned and commanded by Sung Jinwoo through his shadow extraction and control abilities. Death Knights appear throughout various gates and dungeons as formidable obstacles to hunters. Death Knights can be extracted as shadow soldiers to join Sung Jinwoo's growing army of the dead.
41.name: Death Knights

42.content: The Demon Castle Arc represented Sung Jinwoo's venture into a S-Rank instant dungeon created by the System itself. Jinwoo traveled to the Demon Castle with the singular goal of obtaining the recipe for Holy Water of Life, a legendary potion capable of curing his mother's Eternal Slumber. Inside the castle, Jinwoo fought his way through numerous demons and obtained the recipe from defeating powerful creatures. He also encountered the demon noble Esil Radiru, who offered her assistance in exchange for sparing her clan when he faced the Demon King, Baran, the final boss of the dungeon. The Holy Water of Life recipe became one of the three essential ingredients Jinwoo needed to save his mother's life.
42.name: Demon Castle Arc

43.content: The Demon King's Daggers are a pair of magical weapons originally wielded by Baran during his existence as the Demon King. These dual daggers channel dark magical energy and are capable of delivering precise and powerful slashing attacks. The daggers represent high-tier supernatural weapons of extreme rarity, crafted from materials that embody demonic power and magical properties far beyond conventional weapons.
43.name: Demon King's Daggers

44.content: The Demon King's Longsword is a legendary weapon wielded by Baran, the Demon King, within the Demon Castle dungeon. The sword is crafted from rare supernatural materials and possesses immense magical power, capable of delivering devastating attacks and channeling dark magical energy. After Sung Jinwoo defeated Baran on the 100th floor of the Demon Castle, Jinwoo acquired the Demon King's Longsword as a trophy and added it to his collection of powerful magical weapons.
44.name: Demon King's Longsword

45.content: Demons are entities from beyond human understanding that serve the Outer Gods, collectively known as Apostles. Demons appear in various forms and levels of power throughout the world. Demons seek to corrupt hunters and spread their influence through pacts and possession. Demons are responsible for major incidents and pursue hunters of significance like Sung Jinwoo. Demons can grant power to hunters willing to become hosts or servants in exchange for spreading chaos.
45.name: Demons

46.content: The Double Dungeon Arc marked Sung Jinwoo's catastrophic encounter with a hidden dungeon concealed within a D-Rank gate. While raiding what appeared to be a standard D-Rank dungeon with his strike squad, Jinwoo and his companions discovered a concealed second gate inside and decided to investigate. The hidden dungeon turned out to be a deadly trap, locking the hunters inside the Cartenon Temple with a series of animate statues that killed anyone who made even the slightest error. Nearly all members of Jinwoo's raid party were slaughtered before he managed to escape, emerging three days later severely wounded. This traumatic event fundamentally changed Jinwoo, teaching him not to trust others blindly and to prioritize his own survival above all else.
46.name: Double Dungeon Arc

47.content: Dwarves are a creature species that exist within the Solo Leveling world. Dwarves appear in various story contexts throughout the narrative. Dwarves possess their own culture and territories. Dwarves interact with hunters and other creatures within dungeon systems. Dwarves are classified among the races that inhabit the world alongside humans and other sentient species.
47.name: Dwarves

48.content: Elvenwood is an ancient forest region that exists as a parallel dimension and serves as the homeland of the Elves, one of the intelligent races affected by Mana saturation in the human world. The forest contains numerous magical creatures and resources, including contaminated Elvenwood fruits that are highly toxic to humans despite their nutritional value to magical beings. Elvenwood represents a secondary world separate from the human realm where non-human civilizations developed their own societies and cultures in response to the Mana surge orchestrated by the Rulers.
48.name: Elvenwood

49.content: Essence Stones are magical items dropped by powerful monsters and bosses within dungeons, serving as the primary form of currency and resource for hunters. These stones contain concentrated magical energy and are used to create potion ingredients, enhance equipment, and upgrade magical items. High-rank dungeon bosses produce higher-quality essence stones that are significantly more valuable, and rare essence stones from S-Rank dungeons command premium prices in the hunter black market.
49.name: Essence Stones

50.content: Giants are massive humanoid creatures capable of incredible destruction. Giants emerged during the Tokyo S-Rank Gate dungeon break, causing widespread devastation. Giants wield enormous swords and can destroy magical barriers with single strikes. Giants destroyed Yuri Orloff's barrier and swallowed him whole during the Tokyo Crisis. Giants were among the most dangerous creatures encountered, requiring multiple S-Rank hunters to combat.
50.name: Giants

51.content: Gina is a Korean A-Rank Hunter under the Hunters Guild. Gina is a young woman with red eyes, strawberry blonde hair, and a large bust who wears distinctive black armor with red trims. Gina is portrayed as a serious and no-nonsense woman known for her direct approach and displays a streak of recklessness. Gina possesses telekinesis magic allowing her to control and launch objects with considerable effort. Despite only being A-Rank, Gina has sharp senses and was able to correctly realize that Sung Jinwoo was at least A-Rank the first time she met him.
51.name: Gina

52.content: Goto Ryuji was the strongest hunter in Japan and the Guild Master of the Draw Sword Guild. Goto Ryuji was a handsome and muscular man with shaggy black hair and a neatly trimmed black beard whose eyes glowed pure white when using his powers. Goto Ryuji presented himself as calm and collected but was actually consumed by overconfidence and had a cruel disregard for human life, deliberately leading Korean S-Rank Hunters to their deaths. Goto Ryuji's pride prevented him from accepting that Sung Jinwoo was stronger than him. Goto Ryuji was beheaded by the Ant King after refusing to acknowledge he was outmatched.
52.name: Goto Ryuji

53.content: High Elves are magical humanoid creatures that inhabit special barrier settlements established in North Korea. High Elves feed on and are sustained by Elvenwood trees that drain human vitality. High Elves have created refuge areas with Elvenwood where humans are harvested for energy. High Elves were discovered by Sung Suho, Cha Hae-In, and Sirka who destroyed barrier settlements. High Elves are partners with the Apostle of Paradise in maintaining these terrainium settlements.
53.name: High Elves

54.content: Hwang Dongsoo was a Korean-American S-Rank Hunter and the younger brother of Hwang Dongsuk. Hwang Dongsoo was a large and muscular man with black hair in a buzz cut whose eyes glowed bright red when agitated or using his powers. Hwang Dongsoo was a selfish and murderous meathead with little respect for human life, consumed by overconfidence and believing he could defeat opponents obviously stronger than him. Hwang Dongsoo kidnapped Yoo Jinho seeking revenge for his brother's death at Sung Jinwoo's hands. Hwang Dongsoo was killed by Sung Jinwoo and extracted as the shadow soldier Greed.
54.name: Hwang Dongsoo

55.content: The Instant Dungeon Arc represented Sung Jinwoo's first solo hunt after receiving the System. Jinwoo obtained a key to an E-Rank instant dungeon and ventured into it alone, marking the beginning of his independent hunting career. This raid demonstrated Jinwoo's newfound capacity to clear dungeons without relying on a strike squad, while also providing early opportunities to level up through the System's gameplay mechanics. The success of this solo expedition proved that the System's enhancements made Jinwoo far more capable than his E-Rank status suggested.
55.name: Instant Dungeon Arc

56.content: The International Guild Conference Arc saw Sung Jinwoo invited to America as Korea's representative to the prestigious International Guild Conference, a gathering of the world's most powerful guilds and hunters. During the conference, the Monarchs initiated their long-awaited rematch against the Rulers by assassinating Christopher Reed, one of the seven human vessels serving as the Rulers' representatives on Earth. This arc marked the convergence of international politics and the ancient cosmic war between these transcendent forces, with Jinwoo caught at the intersection of both conflicts.
56.name: International Guild Conference Arc

57.content: Japan is an island nation in East Asia that maintains an alliance with South Korea regarding hunter cooperation and dungeon-clearing operations. Japanese hunters, including S-Rank hunters like those from Japanese guilds, have participated in major international raids such as the Jeju Island raid, demonstrating Japan's hunting prowess on the global stage. Japan operates several A-Rank and S-Rank gates within its territory, with Tokyo hosting significant dungeons and a strong hunting infrastructure similar to South Korea.
57.name: Japan

58.content: The Japan Crisis Arc erupted when a S-Rank gate suddenly manifested in Tokyo, overwhelming the strike squad assembled by the Japanese government. The Japanese S-Rank hunters proved incapable of handling the threat posed by the gate's interior, leading to widespread panic and casualties. Sung Jinwoo intervened at Jinah's suggestion, traveling to Tokyo and personally clearing the gate with overwhelming power. This arc cemented Jinwoo's status as a global power broker, demonstrating that even the world's other S-Rank hunters were vastly inferior to him.
58.name: Japan Crisis Arc

59.content: Jonas was the strongest hunter in Brazil, ranking 6th in the world. Jonas was a young man with tan skin, long black dreadlocks, and a black tattoo on his upper right side of his chest. Jonas was originally lined up to attend the International Guild Conference as Brazil's representative but mysteriously disappeared shortly before the event. Jonas was killed by the Monarchs before the International Guild Conference could take place. Jonas was granted power by the Rulers and wielded Ruler's Authority to control objects via telekinesis.
59.name: Jonas

60.content: The Knights Guild is a major hunter organization operating in South Korea that ranks among the top five national guilds. The guild maintains a well-organized structure with multiple specialized teams handling different types of dungeon operations and raid missions. The Knights Guild possesses significant military strength through its roster of skilled hunters and participates actively in national defense efforts and commercial dungeon clearing operations.
60.name: Knights Guild

61.content: Lennart Niermann is the strongest hunter in Germany, ranking 12th in the world. Lennart Niermann is a handsome young man with blue eyes and blonde hair whose eyes glow yellow when using his powers. Lennart Niermann is not particularly arrogant despite being the strongest in his homeland, displaying politeness and patience. Lennart Niermann was able to sense the presence of Sung Jinwoo's massive army of shadow soldiers concealed within his shadow. Lennart Niermann participated in battles against the Monarchs including an encounter with Rakan, and answered the call to fight Antares's armies across Canada.
61.name: Lennart Niermann

62.content: Magic Beasts are creatures that inhabit dungeons and are classified by hunter rank alongside gates. Magic Beasts range from simple creatures like Dungeon Jackals to powerful orc armies and earth giants. Magic Beasts are encountered by hunters during raids and can vary significantly in combat capability and intelligence. Magic Beasts possess magical abilities and physical attributes that make them dangerous opponents. Magic Beasts are slain by hunters for experience and materials needed for progression.
62.name: Magic Beasts

63.content: Mana is the fundamental force of magic that permeates the world after the Rulers and Monarchs began saturating Earth with magical energy approximately 10 years before the main story. Mana radiates from all dungeons and gates, enabling hunters to sense magical beast locations and dungeon entrances. Hunters with greater mana affinity can absorb and utilize mana more effectively, enhancing their physical abilities and magical prowess in direct proportion to the mana concentration in their environment.
63.name: Mana

64.content: Mana Crystals are valuable magical items that accumulate concentrated mana from dungeon environments and magical beings. These crystals serve multiple purposes including magical item crafting, alchemy, and power enhancement for hunters. Higher-purity mana crystals obtained from S-Rank and A-Rank dungeons are exceptionally rare and fetch significant prices, making them highly sought after by alchemists, enchanters, and powerful hunters seeking to augment their magical capabilities.
64.name: Mana Crystals

65.content: The Monarchs War Arc comprised the final, apocalyptic conflict between the Monarchs and the Rulers, fought across the human world with humanity caught in the crossfire. The Monarchs systematically hunted down and murdered multiple Rulers' vessels across different countries, including hunters in Brazil and Korea. Sung Jinwoo became an unintended target and appeared to fall to the Monarchs' onslaught, but their arrogance proved fatal when they failed to completely destroy his corpse. Jinwoo's resurrection and subsequent ascendance to the Shadow Monarch position culminated in a final confrontation that would determine the fate of all humanity.
65.name: Monarchs War Arc

66.content: Orcs are intelligent organized creatures serving under leaders like Kargalgan in various dungeons. Orcs range from common orcs to High Orcs commanding significant combat power. Orcs demonstrated coordinated tactics including the ability to restrain and control large groups of hunters. Orcs appear in Hunters Guild gates and other high-ranking dungeons as major obstacles. Orcs serve under the Architect and can be extracted as shadow soldiers by Sung Jinwoo.
66.name: Orcs

67.content: Park Heejin is a Korean B-Rank Hunter under the White Tiger Guild and one of the five survivors of the Red Gate Incident. Park Heejin is a young woman with bright green eyes and long black hair who wielded a small yellow staff during the Red Gate crisis. Park Heejin is a clever girl who does not lose her cool easily and is smart enough to pick her best chance for survival. Park Heejin chose to stick with Sung Jinwoo for her own safety rather than attempting to clear the dungeon with Kim Chul. Park Heejin is loyal to her guild and has shown the ability to correctly assess hunters' power levels quickly.
67.name: Park Heejin

68.content: The Recruitment Arc occurred in the aftermath of Min Byung-Gyu's funeral, when Sung Jinwoo met Norma Selner, an upgrader who worked directly for the American government. Norma approached Jinwoo to discuss potential recruitment opportunities and to establish ties between South Korea's hunter community and American interests. This encounter marked the beginning of international political intrigue surrounding Jinwoo's growing strength and influence. The meeting demonstrated how Jinwoo's reputation had expanded far beyond Korea, with foreign governments now seeking to secure alliances with or gain leverage over the world's strongest hunter.
68.name: Recruitment Arc

69.content: Siddharth Bachchan was the strongest hunter in India and one of the five National Level Hunters, ranking 4th in the world. Siddharth Bachchan was described as someone with a strong savior complex and incredibly ambitious in his desire to elevate himself as a hunter. Siddharth Bachchan regained his powers and status in the revised timeline, believing himself divinely chosen. Siddharth Bachchan became willing host to Apostles seeking power, attempting to capture Sung Jinwoo to offer to them. Siddharth Bachchan was ultimately consumed by the flames of the Dragon King's Heart and turned into the shadow Sita by Sung Suho.
69.name: Siddharth Bachchan

70.content: South Korea is a nation in East Asia where the majority of Solo Leveling's events occur. The country has become the epicenter of hunters and dungeons, with multiple S-Rank hunters emerging from its borders including Sung Jinwoo, Cha Hae-In, and Choi Jong-In. Major cities including Seoul serve as bases for the largest guilds and hunter organizations, and the Korean Hunters Association oversees all hunting activities within the nation. South Korea's military strength relies heavily on its top-tier hunters due to the frequent appearance of high-ranking gates and dungeons.
70.name: South Korea

71.content: Sung Suho is the son of Sung Jinwoo and Cha Hae-In, and the main protagonist of Solo Leveling: Ragnarok. Sung Suho is a tall, handsome, muscular young man with gray eyes and black hair inherited from his father, whose eyes glow purple when using his powers. Sung Suho possesses immense strength, speed, and durability, and can generate red and black flames to infuse into attacks. Sung Suho drives to become stronger comes from his wish to reunite with his parents after they abruptly vanished when he was a child. Sung Suho commands a shadow army, wields shadow extraction and exchange abilities, and can manifest the Dragon King's Heart for devastating destructive power.
71.name: Sung Suho

72.content: Undead are a group of monster species possessing ghostly, skeletal, and supernatural characteristics ruled by Ashborn, the King of the Dead and Monarch of Shadows. Undead include various types such as Shadows, Arch Liches, Death Knights, Mummies, Vampires, Skeletons, Zombies, Spectres, and Banshees. Undead can be extracted as shadow soldiers and added to Sung Jinwoo's growing army. Undead serve under Ashborn's command within their own hierarchies and territories. Undead interact with the living world through gates and dungeon breaks causing devastation.
72.name: Undead

73.content: The USA is a major global superpower that maintains significant military and hunter strength. American S-Rank hunters like Thomas Andre, the Goliath, represent the nation's peak hunter capability and military prowess. The United States maintains strategic alliances with countries like South Korea and Japan, regularly coordinating on international hunter operations and dungeon raids. America's hunter system and military might make it one of the most influential nations in the geopolitical balance of power during the age of hunters and dungeons.
73.name: USA

74.content: Woo Jinchul is a Korean A-Rank Hunter and the current Chairman of the Korean Hunters Association. Woo Jinchul is a young man with slick dirty blonde hair and black eyes that glow purple when using his abilities. Woo Jinchul is a very stoic man who rarely shows emotion and prefers to stick only to facts. Woo Jinchul served as Chief Inspector of the Hunters Association's Surveillance Team under Go Gunhee before being promoted to Chairman. Woo Jinchul regained his memories of the original timeline in the revised era and has worked to rebuild the Hunters Association and prepare for approaching threats beyond Earth.
74.name: Woo Jinchul

75.content: The World Tree is a legendary ancient entity that exists as a dimension and legendary location shrouded in mystery and myth. The tree represents one of the most significant locations in the supernatural world, with immense magical power concentrated within it. Fragments and leaves of the World Tree are highly prized magical items due to their extraordinary rarity and powerful properties, making any item derived from the tree an object of extreme value to hunters and magical organizations worldwide.
75.name: World Tree

76.content: Yoo Soohyun is a famous Korean actress and model, Yoo Jinho's cousin, and an A-Rank Hunter under the Ahjin Guild. Yoo Soohyun is an attractive young woman with long silky brown hair and big brown eyes who later dyes her hair to a pale shade of purple to match her altered eye color. Yoo Soohyun is affable and charming with a good relationship with Jinho despite being annoyed by his dorky antics. Yoo Soohyun is deeply disdainful towards those with egotistical tendencies and takes great pleasure in watching them get humiliated in public. Yoo Soohyun joined the Ahjin Guild as its third member after learning Sung Jinwoo was involved.
76.name: Yoo Soohyun

77.content: Yuri Orloff was Russia's strongest S-Rank Hunter specialized in support magic, specifically in barriers. Yuri Orloff was an obese middle-aged man with green eyes, messy brown hair, a beer belly, and a thick beard whose eyes glowed teal when using his powers. Yuri Orloff was an arrogant and overconfident individual with a constant craving for attention and booze who demanded $10M USD a day for his services. Yuri Orloff created a magical barrier to block the S-Rank Gate in Tokyo but it was destroyed in a single strike by a giant who then swallowed him whole. In the revised timeline, Yuri regained his powers and rose to become Prime Minister of Russia, using barrier magic and dark powers for ambitious schemes.
77.name: Yuri Orloff

78.content: The Black Market is an underground trading network where hunters and mercenaries buy and sell rare magical items, essence stones, and restricted weapons outside official guild channels. The market operates in the shadows of major cities, accessible only to those with connections or knowledge of its locations. Black Market transactions involve no taxation or official oversight, allowing hunters to trade high-value items and acquire dangerous weapons unavailable through legitimate sources.
78.name: Black Market

79.content: The Fame Guild is a moderate-ranked guild operating in South Korea that competes with larger organizations for dungeon clearing contracts and hunter recruitment. The guild maintains a roster of B-Rank and C-Rank hunters capable of handling standard dungeon operations. Fame Guild's operations focus on commercial dungeon clearing and maintaining territorial control within specific regions of South Korea's hunter market.
79.name: Fame Guild

80.content: Goblins are humanoid magical creatures that inhabit various dungeons throughout the Solo Leveling world. Goblins possess intelligence and basic tool usage, ranging from simple warriors to specialized variant types. Goblins appear across multiple dungeon tiers and serve as common encounters for hunters seeking progression and materials. Various goblin subspecies demonstrate different combat capabilities and tactical organization.
80.name: Goblins

81.content: Holy Water of Life is a legendary potion capable of curing otherwise incurable illnesses and curses, including the Eternal Slumber affliction. Crafting the potion requires three rare ingredients: the tears of a True Poison Fang, a horn from a Lesser Dragon, and a final ingredient obtained from within the Demon Castle. Sung Jinwoo spent considerable effort acquiring these ingredients to cure his mother's condition, making the Holy Water of Life one of the most significant items in the narrative.
81.name: Holy Water of Life

82.content: The Hunters Guild Gate Arc found Sung Jinwoo joining a mining team under the Hunters Guild while awaiting his S-Rank hunter license. This arc provided Jinwoo with direct experience in A-Rank dungeon raids, allowing him to test his abilities against stronger opponents and monsters. The experience helped establish Jinwoo as a formidable force even among established S-Rank hunters.
82.name: Hunters Guild Gate Arc

83.content: Ice Giants are massive humanoid creatures infused with ice magic inhabiting frozen dungeons and tundra regions. Ice Giants command elemental ice powers allowing them to lower temperatures, create ice formations, and launch freezing attacks. Ice Giants possess immense physical size and strength complemented by their magical cryogenic abilities. These creatures represent A-Rank level threats requiring specialized tactics to overcome.
83.name: Ice Giants

84.content: Jisan Prison is a dungeon located at Jisan Mountain that was discovered and classified as a B-Rank dungeon by the Hunters Association. The dungeon contained numerous aggressive monsters and served as a testing ground for mid-tier hunters seeking to improve their combat abilities. The prison's confined layout and concentrated monster population made it a challenging but valuable resource for hunter training and progression.
84.name: Jisan Prison

85.content: Lee Joohee is a Korean hunter affiliated with the White Tiger Guild. Joohee is a skilled mage with ice-type magic abilities. Joohee first encountered Sung Jinwoo during dungeon raids and developed complex feelings toward him, experiencing moments of uncertainty about whether her emotions were romantic love or sympathy rooted in their shared experiences as weak hunters. Joohee maintained respect and affection for Jinwoo despite the vast difference in their power levels as his strength rapidly increased.
85.name: Lee Joohee

86.content: Min Byung-Gyu was a Korean S-Rank Healer and close friend of Baek Yoonho, known for his compassionate nature and distaste for violence. Byung-Gyu was a thin young man with spiky black hair and pale skin who retired early to pursue becoming a history teacher, traumatized by his experiences in three failed Jeju Island raids that claimed his comrade Eunseok. Despite his reluctance, Byung-Gyu sacrificed himself during the Fourth Jeju Island Raid to heal his teammates while under assault by the Ant King, and was subsequently revived as Sung Jinwoo's shadow soldier to save Cha Hae-In before being allowed to rest in peace.
86.name: Min Byung-Gyu

87.content: Minotaurs are powerful humanoid creatures with bovine heads that inhabit dungeons and maze-like structures. Minotaurs demonstrate exceptional physical strength and endurance in combat situations. Minotaurs use their horns and brute force as primary combat tactics against hunters. These creatures are classified among the more dangerous magical beasts hunters encounter.
87.name: Minotaurs

88.content: Nagas are serpentine magical creatures encountered in high-ranking dungeons throughout the Solo Leveling world. Nagas possess humanoid upper bodies with serpentine lower halves and demonstrate considerable intelligence and magical ability. Nagas serve as formidable boss-tier creatures requiring coordinated hunter teams to defeat. These creatures exhibit strategic thinking and can utilize venom and constriction as combat techniques.
88.name: Nagas

89.content: The Retesting Rank Arc occurred after Sung Jinwoo obtained two of the three ingredients necessary to craft the Holy Water of Life. Jinwoo returned to the human world to undergo an official rank re-evaluation, seeking to increase his hunter rank from his initial low standing to reflect his actual combat capabilities. This arc demonstrated Jinwoo's systematic climb through the official hunter ranking system despite his extraordinary power.
89.name: Retesting Rank Arc

90.content: The Return to Demon Castle Arc saw Sung Jinwoo venture back into the Demon Castle to obtain the final ingredient for the Holy Water of Life. During this expedition, Jinwoo met the demon noble Esil Radiru, who agreed to guide him through the castle's deadly interior and lead him to the Demon King Baran in exchange for mercy toward her clan. The arc culminated in Jinwoo's confrontation with Baran and the successful acquisition of the crucial final ingredient needed to cure his mother's Eternal Slumber.
90.name: Return to Demon Castle Arc

91.content: Rune Stones are magical artifacts inscribed with ancient runes that enhance the abilities of hunters who carry them or channel their power. These stones amplify magical energy and provide various defensive and offensive benefits depending on their inscriptions. Rune Stones are discovered within high-tier dungeons and are highly valued by S-Rank hunters and powerful organizations seeking to maximize their combat potential.
91.name: Rune Stones

92.content: The Scavenger Guild is a hunter organization operating in South Korea that specializes in item collection and resource gathering from dungeons and gates. The guild maintains a workforce of C-Rank and B-Rank hunters focused on efficient monster hunting and loot acquisition rather than high-difficulty raid operations. The Scavenger Guild operates as one of the specialized guilds within South Korea's competitive hunter market.
92.name: Scavenger Guild

93.content: Seoul Station Field is a dungeon entrance that opened near Seoul Station in South Korea, serving as a repeated training ground for hunters. The field contains low to mid-tier monsters suitable for C-Rank and B-Rank hunters seeking experience and loot. Seoul Station Field's accessibility and consistent monster spawn rate made it a popular location for guild-affiliated hunters conducting routine dungeon raids.
93.name: Seoul Station Field

94.content: Sung Jinah is the younger sister of Sung Jinwoo and a civilian who was largely unaware of the dangers hunters face for most of the series. Jinah cares deeply for her brother and became a point of emotional grounding for Jinwoo amid his increasingly perilous adventures. Jinah's safety remained a constant concern for Jinwoo throughout his journey, driving some of his decisions regarding his awakening powers and his efforts to protect humanity from increasingly powerful threats.
94.name: Sung Jinah

95.content: Teleportation Stones are magical items that enable hunters to instantly transport themselves across short to moderate distances, providing tactical advantages in combat and dungeon navigation. These rare artifacts are crafted from specialized magical materials and are highly valued for their utility in emergency situations. Teleportation Stones are typically obtained from S-Rank dungeons or through black market transactions, making them exclusive equipment for elite hunters.
95.name: Teleportation Stone

96.content: Werewolves are hybrid creatures combining humanoid and lupine characteristics, inhabiting dungeons across the Solo Leveling world. Werewolves possess enhanced speed, strength, and predatory instincts compared to ordinary humans. Werewolves operate with pack-like tactics and demonstrate coordinated hunting behavior. These creatures are classified as B-Rank level magical beasts requiring significant hunter capability to defeat.
96.name: Werewolves

97.content: Yoo Jinho is a Korean D-Rank Hunter and famous video game developer who serves as Vice-Guild Master of the Ahjin Guild under Sung Jinwoo. Jinho is a young man with gray eyes, a boyish face, and custard-colored hair, typically wearing casual clothing even during dungeon raids. Despite his weak rank and lack of combat experience, Jinho repeatedly demonstrated unwavering loyalty to Sung Jinwoo, seeing him as an older brother figure and willingly leaving his wealthy family business to help establish the Ahjin Guild. Jinho's genuine nature and friendly demeanor contrasted sharply with other hunters, making him Jinwoo's closest companion throughout the series.
97.name: Yoo Jinho

98.content: The Yoo Jinho Raid Party Arc formed when Sung Jinwoo agreed to team up with Yoo Jinho to establish a legal raid party capable of clearing C-Rank dungeons. Their rapid success in clearing dungeons attracted the attention of Ahn Sangmin, a high-ranking employee of the White Tiger Guild, who became interested in Jinwoo's unusual abilities and quick progression through the hunter ranks.
98.name: Yoo Jinho Raid Party Arc
